svg.text: From four chunkers to a chunking-strategy-aware RAG pipeline (C1M3 Ungraded Lab 2)
1. The four chunkers you need to know

g.text: Note: this lab counts in words, not characters,
so chunk_size=100 means ~100 words per chunk.
+ Words at boundaries appear in 2 chunks (recall↑)
− Headings often land as tiny standalone chunks
Pick the marker that matches the format you have.

svg.text: 2. Wrap each chunk with metadata (and a strategy tag!)

text.tspan: # Later, before insert: tag each chunk with which strategy made it

svg.text: you filter by strategy at query
time → A/B compare side by side.
whole strategy name is one token.
4. The big insight: query type decides the right chunk size

g.text: 25-word chunks may match semantically but lack
context to actually educate the reader.
"how to add the url of a remote repo"
● Larger chunks bury the answer in walls of

svg.text: 5. RAG integration — balance top_k against chunk size

text.tspan: "Using this information and only this information,"
" please explain {search_string} in a few short points.\nContext: {context}"
# Compensate for chunk size: many small chunks, few big chunks
# grab MORE of the small ones
# grab FEWER of the big ones

svg.text: look unfairly bad in the eval.
✓ Filter at query time to A/B test strategies
✓ Tune limit per strategy so context stays comparable
✓ Broad query → bigger chunks ● Specific query → smaller
● Docling / Unstructured • layout-aware for PDFs & tables
● Contextual Retrieval • prepend doc summary per chunk
